members.name: Arabic-Latin-Full-Name-Match
members.description: A production-ready Python library for cross-script fuzzy name matching between Arabic and Latin (French/English) romanised Algerian names. Built for deduplication, record linkage, and identity resolution in Algerian administrative datasets.
members.readme:
Algerian Name Matcher

A production-ready Python library for **cross-script fuzzy name matching** between Arabic and Latin (French/English) romanised Algerian names. Built for deduplication, record linkage, and identity resolution in Algerian administrative datasets.

---

The Problem

Algerian names exist in two competing scripts with no standardised transliteration:

| Arabic | Latin (French) | Latin (English) |
|---|---|---|
| عبد الرحمن بوزيد | Bouzid Abderrahmane | Abderrezak Bouzid |
| هدى بن عيسى | Benaissa Houda | Houda Benaissa |
| زين الدين مخلوفي | Zinedine Makhloufi | Zineddine Makhloufi |

Off-the-shelf fuzzy matchers fail on these because they treat names as bags of characters, letting a shared surname rescue a completely wrong first name — a **collision**.

---

How It Works

Normalisation Pipeline

Every name, regardless of script, passes through the same pipeline and emerges as a single phonetic Latin form before scoring:

 

Scoring Algorithm

 

Protected by two guards:

**Consonant-skeleton gate** — vowels are stripped from both sides before the worst-case bidirectional match is checked. hdy and houda both reduce to hd, so the gate passes correctly even though the raw strings look very different.

**Asymmetry penalty** — if the two names have different word counts after normalisation, the score is scaled down proportionally.

---

Installation

 

> camel-tools requires its data files on first use:
>  

---

Quick Start

 

Single pair comparison

 

Normalise a name

 

Bulk search — find best match in a database

 

---

FastAPI Integration

 

---

Pu...

members.name: DataMatching_ArticleData
members.description: Data Matching Tests with Article Data with the libraries Dedupe.io and Python Record Linkage Toolkit
members.readme:
Test project for data matching with article data

This repository contains python scripts to test the two data matching libraries 

Dedupe
Python Record Linkage Toolkit

The scripts are developed using the use the PyCharm IDE and Python 3.6.

Project structure

Src

This directory contains the scripts.

Tools.py (helper methods and classes)
Evaluation.py (methods to evaluate data matching results)
CompareMethods.py (script to test the various string comparison methods of the Python Record Linkage Toolkit)
Dedupe.py (script to test the data matching using the Dedupe library)
PythonRecordLinkageToolkit.py (script to test the data matching using the Python Record Linkage Toolkit library)

The test scripts need a config file passed as command line argument to run. Examples can be found in the Data directory.

Data

This directory contains test data and test configuration files. 

The test data was downloaded from the Database Group Leipzig. 

Reproducibility

The tests call the *init_random_with_seed* method to set the random number generator to a fixed seed value. 
Additionaly, the PYTHONHASHSEED environment variables must be set for the dedupe.py script.

members.name: jaro-winkler-cli
members.description: Calculate Jaro-Winkler string similarity from the terminal.
members.readme:
@kszongic/jaro-winkler-cli

 
 

Calculate **Jaro-Winkler string similarity** from the command line. Zero dependencies.

The Jaro-Winkler distance is commonly used in record linkage, deduplication, and fuzzy matching. It gives higher scores to strings that match from the beginning — great for comparing names, typos, and near-duplicates.

Install

 

Usage

 

Options

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| -p, --prefix-weight <n> | Prefix scaling factor (default: 0.1, max 0.25) |
| -j, --jaro | Output Jaro distance only (no Winkler boost) |
| --json | Output as JSON |
| -h, --help | Show help |

Examples

 

How It Works

**Jaro distance** counts matching characters (within a window) and transpositions
**Winkler modification** boosts the score for strings sharing a common prefix (up to 4 chars)
Output is a value between 0 (no similarity) and 1 (identical)

License

MIT © 2026 kszongic

members.name: splink_stata
members.description: Probabilistic record linkage for Stata. C plugin implementation of the Fellegi-Sunter model, matching the feature set of Python splink.
members.readme:
splink_stata

Probabilistic record linkage for Stata using the Fellegi-Sunter model. A C plugin implementation matching the feature set of Python splink v4.

Installation

 

Features

**19 comparison methods**: Jaro-Winkler, Jaro, Levenshtein, Damerau-Levenshtein, Jaccard, exact, numeric, cosine, intersect, name, email, postcode, nameswap, dob, pctdiff, abs_date, abs_time, distance_km, custom
**Configurable comparison levels**: variable number of thresholds per field via complevels()
**Multiple blocking rules**: up to 4 rules with OR logic via block(), block2(), block3(), block4(), or arbitrary rules via blockrules()
**EM parameter estimation**: joint estimation of m-probabilities, u-probabilities, and lambda
**Term frequency adjustments**: tfadjust() for frequency-aware weighting with named TF sources
**Cross-dataset linking**: linkvar() + linktype() for deduplication, linking, or both
**Null handling**: neutral (default) or penalize missing values via nullweight()
**Pairwise output**: savepairs() writes CSV with match weights, probabilities, gamma levels, and Bayes factors
**Random u-estimation**: uestimate option for splink-style u-probability estimation from random pairs
**Model save/load**: savemodel() and loadmodel() for JSON model persistence
**Supervised m-estimation**: mlabel() for labeled training data
**Round-robin EM**: roundrobin for per-blocking-rule EM training
**Clustering**: connected components (default) or best-link via clustermethod()
**Cluster thresholding**: separate clusterthreshold() for cluster formation vs pair scoring
**Salting**: salt() for bloc...

unmatched.name: trade-record-linker
unmatched.description: Python record linkage library for noisy customs data. Blocking, comparators, rule-based + ML classifiers.
unmatched.readme:
trade-record-linker

A record linkage and deduplication library for customs trade data. Built for
importer names, addresses, and HS-coded goods descriptions that are often
transliterated across alphabets, abbreviated, or plain noisy.

Why

Customs feeds from multiple declarants, forwarders, and terminal operators
produce divergent strings for the same real-world entity. Manual stitching
does not scale past a few thousand records. This library provides a
blocking + scoring + clustering pipeline that is transparent enough for
customs analysts to audit every match decision.

Pipeline

**Blocking** reduces the O(n^2) pair space. Three blockers ship:
   - NgramBlocker (character n-gram buckets),
   - PhoneticBlocker (Double Metaphone on token heads),
   - JaccardBlocker (token-set jaccard over a shingled representation).
**Comparators** score each candidate pair field-by-field. Included:
   Jaro-Winkler, Levenshtein ratio, set Jaccard, SoftTFIDF, numeric
   closeness, and date closeness.
**Classifier** reduces the per-field vector into a scalar match score.
   The default is a rule-based weighted sum; a SupervisedClassifier
   wraps a scikit-learn estimator for labeled-data workflows.
**Clustering** builds a graph of pairs above threshold and collapses
   connected components. Transitive-closure tightening applies a second
   pass to prune chains held together by a single weak edge.

Install

 

Requires Python 3.11+.

Quickstart

 

CLI

 

The CLI reads a CSV, infers a default schema (importer + address + hs_code
if present), runs the linker, and writes a two-column cluster_id...

unmatched.name: entity-embed
unmatched.description: PyTorch library for transforming entities like companies, products, etc. into vectors to support scalable Record Linkage / Entity Resolution using Approximate Nearest Neighbors.
unmatched.readme:
Entity Embed

 
 
 
 
 
 

Entity Embed allows you to transform entities like companies, products, etc. into vectors to support **scalable Record Linkage / Entity Resolution using Approximate Nearest Neighbors**.

Using Entity Embed, you can train a deep learning model to transform records into vectors in an N-dimensional embedding space. Thanks to a contrastive loss, those vectors are organized to keep similar records close and dissimilar records far apart in this embedding space. Embedding records enables scalable ANN search, which means finding thousands of candidate duplicate pairs of records per second per CPU.

Entity Embed achieves Recall of ~0.99 with Pair-Entity ratio below 100 on a variety of datasets. **Entity Embed aims for high recall at the expense of precision. Therefore, this library is suited for the Blocking/Indexing stage of an Entity Resolution pipeline.**  A scalabale and noise-tolerant Blocking procedure is often the main bottleneck for performance and quality on Entity Resolution pipelines, so this library aims to solve that. Note the ANN search on embedded records returns several candidate pairs that must be filtered to find the best matching pairs, possibly with a pairwise classifier (an example for that is available).

Entity Embed is based on and is a special case of the AutoBlock model described by Amazon.

**⚠️ Warning: this project is under heavy development.**

 

Documentation

https://entity-embed.readthedocs.io

Requirements

System

MacOS or Linux (tested on latest MacOS and Ubuntu via GitHub Actions).
Entity Embed can train and run on a p...

unmatched.name: Record-Deduplication
unmatched.description: Duplication of Record is solved using the Record Linkage library of Python
unmatched.readme:
Record-Deduplication

Python Libraries used:

 Pandas
 
 RecordLinkage

The Python Record Linkage Toolkit is a library to link records in or between data sources. The toolkit provides most of the tools needed for record linkage and deduplication. The package contains indexing methods, functions to compare records and classifiers. The package is developed for research and the linking of small or medium sized files

The term record linkage is used to indicate the procedure of bringing together information from two or more records that are believed to belong to the same entity. Record linkage is used to link data from multiple data sources or to find duplicates in a single data source. In computer science, record linkage is also known as data matching or deduplication (in case of search duplicate records within a single file).

Indexing Method
The Blocking indexing method is used to make the pair of similar records. To run the code on different dataset just replace the column names #block_class = rl.BlockIndex(on=["column names"]) in the following line of the code.

Compare Records

The comparison between data and record pairs is done with the help of record linkage compare class. After comparing, the indexes of the matched data is return.

So, omit the indexed returned from the dataset provided and write the output in the file.

unmatched.name: dqbench
unmatched.description: The standard benchmark for data quality tools — detection, transformation, entity resolution, and pipeline orchestration. 4 categories, 12 tiers, 161 tests.
unmatched.readme:
DQBench

The standard benchmark for data quality tools — detection, transformation, entity resolution, and pipeline orchestration.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

> The ImageNet of data quality — standardized benchmarks for validation, transformation, entity resolution, and pipeline tools.

Why DQBench?

Every data quality tool claims to be the best. But there's no standard way to compare them. DQBench fixes that with:

**Four benchmark categories** — Detect, Transform, ER, and Pipeline
**Three difficulty tiers** — basics, realistic, and adversarial
**Ground truth** — every planted issue is documented with affected rows
**Fair scoring** — recall AND precision matter (no gaming by flagging everything)
**One number** — DQBench Score (0-100) for easy comparison
**20-line integration** — implement one method to benchmark any tool

Install

The repo now also includes an OCR Company benchmark for post-OCR company-name confidence and correction quality.

 

Quick Start

 

Benchmark Categories

| Category | What it measures | Example tools |
|----------|-----------------|---------------|
| **Detect** | Find data quality issues in a dataset | GoldenCheck, Great Expectations, Pandera, Soda Core |
| **Transform** | Clean, normalize, and repair data | GoldenFlow, dbt, pandas |
| **ER** | Entity resolution — deduplicate and link records | GoldenMatch, Splink, Dedupe |
| **Pipeline** | End-to-end pipeline orchestration and quality gates | GoldenPipe, Airflow, Prefect |
| **OCR Company** | OCR company-name confidence, review, and correction quality | OCR confidence/correction tools |

Head-to-Head Resul...

The identity and delegation layer for AI agents.

unmatched.name: tidyname
unmatched.description: Intelligent company name cleaning and normalization for Python. Entity resolution for messy data with fuzzy matching.
unmatched.readme:
TidyName

Intelligent company name cleaning for Python.

TidyName is a Python package that intelligently removes legal entity terms and 
organization type indicators from company names while preserving cases where 
these terms are part of the actual business name.

Features

**Smart Detection**: Identifies and removes corporate suffixes (LLC, Inc., Ltd., etc.)
**Intelligent Preservation**: Preserves terms when they're part of brand names (e.g., "The Limited")
**Confidence Scoring**: Provides confidence levels for each cleaning decision
**International Support**: Handles international corporate suffixes (GmbH, S.A., etc.)
**Batch Processing**: Clean multiple company names efficiently
**Configurable**: Customize behavior through configuration options
**Pure Python**: No external dependencies required
**Type Safety**: Full type annotations for better IDE support

Requirements

Python 3.13+

Installation

 

Quick Start

 

Advanced Usage

Batch Processing

 

Configuration Options

 

Supported Terms

Corporate Suffixes
**Corporation**: Company, Incorporated, Corporation, Corp., Corp, Inc., Inc
**Limited Liability**: LLC, L.L.C., PLC, P.L.C.
**Limited**: Limited, Ltd., Ltd, Co., Co
**Partnership**: & Co., & Co, LLP, L.L.P.
**Professional**: Professional Corporation, P.C., PC

International Suffixes
**German**: GmbH, AG
**French**: S.A., S.A
**Dutch**: N.V., B.V.
**Italian**: S.r.l., S.p.A.

Basic Cleaning

 

Brand Preservation

unmatched.name: dedupe
unmatched.description: :id: A python library for accurate and scalable fuzzy matching, record deduplication and entity-resolution.
unmatched.readme:
Dedupe Python Library

  

_dedupe is a python library that uses machine learning to perform fuzzy matching, deduplication and entity resolution quickly on structured data._

__dedupe__ will help you: 

__remove duplicate entries__ from a spreadsheet of names and addresses
__link a list__ with customer information to another with order history, even without unique customer IDs
take a database of campaign contributions and __figure out which ones were made by the same person__, even if the names were entered slightly differently for each record

dedupe takes in human training data and comes up with the best rules for your dataset to quickly and automatically find similar records, even with very large databases.

Important links
Documentation: https://docs.dedupe.io/
Repository: https://github.com/dedupeio/dedupe
Issues: https://github.com/dedupeio/dedupe/issues
Mailing list: https://groups.google.com/forum/#!forum/open-source-deduplication
Examples: https://github.com/dedupeio/dedupe-examples

dedupe library consulting

If you or your organization would like professional assistance in working with the dedupe library, Dedupe.io LLC offers consulting services. Read more about pricing and available services here.

Tools built with dedupe

Dedupe.io
A cloud service powered by the dedupe library for de-duplicating and finding matches in your data. It provides a step-by-step wizard for uploading your data, setting up a model, training, clustering and reviewing the results.

Dedupe.io also supports record linkage across data sources and continuous matching and training through an A...

unmatched.name: medicine-chatbot
unmatched.description: Intelligent Medicine Search Chatbot. Python script using Pandas and fuzzy string matching (fuzzywuzzy) to provide quick, non-case-sensitive information retrieval from a local drug data file.
unmatched.readme:
💊 Intelligent Medicine Search Chatbot

This is a functional Python command-line tool designed for quick, non-case-sensitive lookups of medicine information from a structured data source.

✨ Features
*Robust Data Loading:* Attempts to load data using multiple common file encodings.
*Fuzzy Matching:* Uses the fuzzywuzzy library to find the closest match for user input, even with spelling mistakes or partial names.
*Structured Output:* Displays retrieved information (Uses, Dosage, Side Effects, etc.) in a clear, formatted console output.

🛠 Requirements
This project requires the following Python libraries:
pandas
fuzzywuzzy
python-Levenshtein (Recommended for faster fuzzy matching)

🚀 How to Run
*Ensure Python 3 is installed.*
*Install dependencies* (or use the requirements.txt file once created):
   ```bash
   pip install pandas fuzzywuzzy python-Levenshtein

mission: Externe Loesungsraum-Landkarte fuer "Derselbe physische Event wird von mehreren Quellen eingesammelt — mit leicht unterschiedlichen Titeln ("Jazz im Keller" vs" ins Zielprojekt eventbear-worker ueberfuehren
Divergente Cluster (1) pruefen — hoeheres Lernpotenzial, hoeherer Transfer-Aufwand
deliverable: Je Cluster ein Kurz-Exposé mit Key-Takeaway fuer das Zielprojekt
Pro divergentem Cluster eine klare adopt/adapt/observe/ignore-Entscheidung
Decision-Log-Eintrag(e) im Zielprojekt, die auf diese Landscape referenzieren
context: Aktueller Ansatz: Simple String-Match auf Titel + Datum. Funktioniert bei ~60% der Fälle; der Rest bleibt Dublette oder wird falsch gemerged. Keine Fuzzy-Logic, keine Adress-Normalisierung im Matcher, keine Zeit-Toleranz.

Erfolgskriterium: Zwei Varianten desselben Events werden zu 1 kanonischer Entity gemerged,Zwei verschiedene Events desselben Veranstalters bleiben getrennt (keine False-Merges),Merge-Entscheidungen inspectable mit pro-Feld Begründung,Konflikt-Resolution bei Merge (welche Werte gewinnen) deterministisch
Approach-Signature: record-linkage, entity-resolution, fuzzy-matching, probabilistic-matching, blocking, similarity-scoring
Tech-Tags: nodejs, typescript, javascript, python, java, node
guardrails: Matching ohne zentrale ID — nur via Inhalt und Metadaten
uncertainties: Nicht-Ziel: Kein manuelles Merge-Review als Default-Flow — auto-merge bei hoher Confidence
Nicht-Ziel: Kein DB-natives Merging (Postgres extensions o.ä.) — muss in Node-Worker laufen
Nicht-Ziel: Keine ML-Model-Training-Pipeline — existierende Heuristik + Libraries

primary.implementationGoal: Adaptions-Skizze fuer Cluster "jaro-winkler+python+string-similarity" als Bruecke zum Zielprojekt
primary.firstSlice: Minimal-Integration: 1 Vertreter aus Cluster "jaro-winkler+python+string-similarity" gegen das Zielprojekt testen
compareChecklist: Wie unterscheidet sich dieser divergente Ansatz vom aktuellen Stand?
Welche Annahmen des aktuellen Ansatzes stellt dieses Cluster in Frage?
Ist der Transfer-Aufwand in Relation zum erwarteten Gewinn vertretbar?
stopIf: Performance oder Qualitaet unter dem aktuellen Niveau ohne klare Perspektive
Lizenz oder Distributionsform passt nicht zum Zielprojekt

compareChecklist: Wo unterscheidet sich dieser Pfad vom primaeren?
stopIf: Keine klaren Unterschiede zum primaeren Pfad

compareChecklist: Wo unterscheidet sich dieser Pfad vom primaeren?
stopIf: Keine klaren Unterschiede zum primaeren Pfad
